nodes.en: Fabrication and refinement of implements and mechanical systems—including metallurgy, woodworking, and assembly of hand tools. Presupposes basic energy availability and raw materials; supports agriculture, construction, and specialized crafts; distinct from chemistry by focusing on mechanical form rather than molecular transformation.
nodes.cs: Výroba a zlepšování nástrojů a mechanických systémů—včetně metalurgie, zpracování dřeva a složení ručních nástrojů. Předpokládá dostupnost základní energie a surovin; podporuje zemědělství, stavby a specializované řemeslo; odlišuje se od chemie zaměřením na mechanickou formu namísto molekulární transformace.
nodes.brief:
Tool Making is the fabrication and maintenance of hand tools and simple machines — forging, casting, woodworking, and mechanical assembly — that converts raw or salvaged materials into the implements every other rebuilding effort depends on. It presupposes the energy and materials [[12.01]] Basic Infrastructure supplies and, in turn, produces the instruments that make [[12.03]] Essential Chemistry's apparatus and [[12.04]] Basic Medicine Production's equipment buildable; unlike those siblings, it works mechanical form rather than molecular or biological transformation, which is why none of the other rebuilding subfields can progress without a working toolkit first.

Elsewhere in the corpus, [[07.06]] Welding and [[03.05.01.03]] Machining cover the same joining and material-removal techniques at industrial precision and scale. Tool Making adapts the same underlying metallurgy and craft to what can be built and repaired by hand with a simple forge, salvaged stock, and no supply chain of replacement parts.

For technique and buildable designs, [Open Source Ecology's Global Village Construction Set](https://www.opensourceecology.org/gvcs/) documents open-hardware plans for the core machines a small group needs to bootstrap fabrication, and the [Wikipedia entry on blacksmithing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Blacksmith) covers the foundational hand-forging techniques this subfield draws on.
